finding_results.notes: The overheard deck exchange now adds new leverage beyond the meeting time: Cliff names the forty-thousand-dollar issue, makes the demand for Tommy's backing explicit, threatens to bring it into the house, and Tommy agrees only on the condition that Cliff keep it off the table. Nate still has the phone running through the beat.
Paul's line is contracted to match the scene's natural pressure, and Tommy's reply now uses contracted forms consistent with his high-contraction, short-declarative voice.

Tommy's three-line run (119, 125, 133) now uses contractions in all three lines and grounds the meaning in concrete roster detail rather than the abstract placeholder 'the thing.' Line 125 replaces 'You're not hearing the thing' with 'look, he sat in that chair and picked every one of those guys. You don't come in after and fix his picks.' Line 133 replaces 'No one gets to improve Martin now. That's the thing' with 'He's done. His lineup's his lineup. Nobody gets to make it better for him now.' Both are blunter, more contracted, and anchored in the draft rather than abstract diagnosis. Paul's line 269 replaces the aphoristic 'For him that was love' with 'I think for him that was the same thing... The control and--yeah. Showing up.' -- adding Paul's characteristic qualifier, a trailing self-correction, and a more lived-in phrasing that still lands the meaning (Martin experienced control as care) without arriving as a clean thesis. Nate's narration at 127 and Joel's prompt at 129 were adjusted to match the revised Tommy dialogue.

Re-threaded Nate's subjectivity through both long exterior stretches with bodily reaction, private inference, and tactile contact with the recording phone so the dinner scene stays Nate-locked without breaking the table-talk rhythm.

finding_results.notes: Rebuilt the packet-named reaction beats away from the repeated 'That ...' opener and varied them through laughter, movement, glassware, and Nate's sensory read of the room.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised the chapter-ending recorded exchange so it carries fresh pressure and terms instead of repeating the dock appointment, and corrected the Paul/Tommy contraction drift in the lineup-management scene.

Single finding addressed: Tommy's emotional-clarification lines and Paul's summary line were reworked for idiolect fidelity. Tommy's abstract 'the thing' phrasing was replaced with concrete roster detail grounded in the draft itself, and his uncontracted stiffness was resolved. Paul's aphoristic 'For him that was love' was replaced with a qualified, trailing observation that preserves the meaning through Paul's measured voice rather than a tidy diagnosis. Surrounding narration and Joel's prompt were adjusted to match. All changes are local to the two cited spans; no structural, continuity, or downstream impact.
Chapter 13 now keeps close-third pressure on Nate through the dinner scene and no longer relies on the repeated reaction-paragraph scaffold at the packet's flagged beats.
